title: Deadliest Day: Mass Killings Under Blackout
detail: Under cover of the nationwide internet blackout, security forces carry out mass killings in Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, and Kermanshah. Later reporting from Time, The Guardian, and Iran International will estimate between 30,000 and 36,500 killed on January 8-9 combined. January 9 is documented as the single deadliest day, with IRGC and Basij forces using automatic weapons, vehicle-mounted machine guns, and in some reports helicopter gunships against crowds.

media.caption: Mass killings under internet blackout as security forces use automatic weapons against protesters

title: Tehran's Southern Suburbs Bear Heaviest Toll
detail: The densely populated working-class neighborhoods of south Tehran — including Shahr-e Rey, Islamshahr, and Robat Karim — see the worst violence. Witnesses who later escaped describe security forces firing indiscriminately into apartment buildings and crowds gathered in streets. Bodies are reportedly loaded onto military trucks and removed before dawn.

media.caption: Tehran's southern suburbs bear heaviest toll as security forces fire indiscriminately

title: Isfahan and Shiraz Report Hundreds Dead
detail: Fragmentary reports filtering through the internet blackout indicate mass casualties in Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square and surrounding neighborhoods, as well as in Shiraz's Karimkhan district. Medical sources later confirm hospitals were overwhelmed and ordered to turn away wounded protesters. Morgues in both cities run out of capacity.

media.caption: Isfahan and Shiraz report hundreds dead as hospitals are overwhelmed and morgues run out of capacity

title: International Alarm as Reports Trickle Out
detail: Despite the internet blackout, satellite phone reports and accounts from those near borders begin to paint a picture of unprecedented violence. The US State Department issues an urgent statement expressing 'grave concern.' The UK Foreign Secretary calls the situation 'potentially the worst atrocity of the 21st century.' UN Secretary-General calls for immediate ceasefire and access for investigators.

media.caption: International alarm grows as reports of mass killings trickle out despite internet blackout
